title: Fluffy Norwegian Dum Bunad
description: A peppery and fluffy Norwegian dish featuring chilli flakes and dum, perfect for a lazy Sunday morning. This egg-free-friendly recipe is a delicious twist on traditional Norwegian cuisine.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, warm water, salt, sugar, and yeast to form a sticky dough.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium heat.
Divide the dough into 6-8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball slightly into a disk shape.
Heat a non-stick pan or griddle over medium heat and cook the dum for 2-3 minutes on each side, or until they are golden brown.
Serve the Fluffy Norwegian Dum Bunad warm with your favorite toppings, such as applesauce and sesame seeds.
